tasks.title: Generate country territories during world generation (Voronoi partitioning)
tasks.description: The world hierarchy schema already has geographic coordinate fields (mapWidth, mapDepth, mapCenter on worlds; position, territoryPolygon, territoryRadius on countries; position, boundaryPolygon on states; worldPositionX, worldPositionZ, radius on settlements) — but no generation logic populates them. Implement territory generation during world creation. (1) In the world generator, compute mapWidth/mapDepth from the configured world scale and number of countries. (2) Assign each country a position within the world bounds, spaced to avoid overlap. (3) Generate non-overlapping country territoryPolygon using Voronoi partitioning (or similar) so territories tile the world area with no gaps. Consider using d3-delaunay or a simple Fortune's algorithm — cell count is small (1-10 countries). (4) Territory shapes should be influenced by terrain — rivers and mountain ranges can form natural borders where possible. (5) Compute country position (centroid of polygon) and territoryRadius (max distance from centroid to any vertex). (6) Persist all fields to the database during generation. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Place settlements within country territories with terrain-aware positioning
tasks.description: Settlements need to be placed at real coordinates inside their parent country's territory. (1) Use rejection sampling to place settlements at terrain-appropriate positions within their country's territoryPolygon — river settlements near water features, mountain settlements at elevation, coastal settlements at territory edges. (2) Persist worldPositionX/worldPositionZ to the database for each settlement. (3) Persist settlement radius from existing BASE_RADIUS in boundary-generator (hamlet=50, village=80, town=150, city=250, scaled by population). (4) Enforce minimum distance between settlements (sum of their radii + buffer) to prevent overlap. (5) Offset settlement boundaryPolygon vertices by the world position (local coords → world coords). (6) Generate inter-settlement roads connecting settlements within the same country — store as world-level street/road segments. Can reuse the existing RoadGenerator pattern. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Subdivide country territories into state/province regions
tasks.description: When a country has states/provinces, its territory needs to be subdivided into non-overlapping state boundaries. (1) If the country has states, subdivide the country territoryPolygon into non-overlapping state boundaries using Voronoi or similar subdivision. (2) Each state's boundaryPolygon must fit within the parent country's territoryPolygon. (3) Each state's settlements must be positioned within that state's boundary. (4) Set state position to the centroid of its boundary polygon. (5) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Unify street generation types: adapter from StreetGenerator edges to StreetNetwork segments
tasks.description: See docs/TERRAIN_STREET_PATTERNS.md for full analysis. Two parallel street generation systems exist: street-network-generator.ts (active, grid-only, outputs {nodes, segments}) and street-generator.ts (orphaned, 6 patterns, outputs {nodes, edges}). Write an adapter function that converts StreetGenerator's output ({nodes: StreetNode[], edges: StreetEdge[]} from shared/game-engine/types.ts) into the StreetNetwork format used by street-network-generator.ts ({nodes: StreetNode[], segments: StreetSegment[]}). (1) Map each StreetEdge to a StreetSegment, preserving waypoints, name, width, direction, and node references. (2) Convert StreetNode types between the two schemas (the active system uses {id, x, z, intersectionOf} while the orphaned uses {id, x, z, type, connections}). (3) Ensure the converted output is compatible with geography-generator.ts line 217 (segment-to-Location conversion) and placeLots(). (4) Export the adapter from street-network-generator.ts. (5) Write unit tests verifying round-trip fidelity for each pattern type. (6) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Wire terrain-aware pattern selection into geography generator
tasks.description: See docs/TERRAIN_STREET_PATTERNS.md. Currently geography-generator.ts:214 calls generateStreetNetwork() which always returns grid. Update it to select the street pattern based on terrain and settlement type, using StreetGenerator.selectStreetPattern() rules: coast→waterfront, river→linear, mountains→hillside, village/pre-1800→organic, city<10k→radial, default→grid. (1) Add terrain to StreetNetworkConfig interface. (2) In generateStreetNetwork(), when terrain triggers a non-grid pattern, instantiate StreetGenerator, call its generate() method, and convert the output using the adapter from the previous task. (3) For the grid pattern, continue using the existing generateGridNetwork(). (4) Pass the selected pattern name through to the return value so geography-generator can log it. (5) Store the pattern type on the settlement record so the client knows the layout style. (6) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Implement lot placement for non-grid street patterns
tasks.description: See docs/TERRAIN_STREET_PATTERNS.md. The existing placeLots() in street-network-generator.ts only handles rectangular grid blocks (3×2 lots per block). Non-grid patterns produce irregular street graphs that need different lot placement strategies. (1) For linear pattern: place lots in rows along both sides of the main street and side streets, with lot depth perpendicular to each street edge. (2) For waterfront pattern: place lots in curved rows following the shoreline streets, with lots facing the water on the shore side and facing inland on the other. (3) For organic pattern: compute irregular block polygons from the street graph (cycle detection or face extraction), then scatter lots within each polygon using rejection sampling. (4) For radial pattern: place lots along each radiating street and around each concentric ring, with lot facing angles pointing toward the center. (5) For hillside pattern: place lots in terraced rows along each contour street, with lots facing downhill. (6) All patterns must produce LotPlacement[] compatible with the existing interface (x, z, streetId, streetName, houseNumber, side, facingAngle, lotWidth, lotDepth, zone). (7) Identify the park/town square block for each pattern and mark it with zone='park'. (8) Write unit tests for each pattern confirming lot count scales with settlement type. (9) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Update client-side rendering to handle non-grid street layouts from DB
tasks.description: See docs/TERRAIN_STREET_PATTERNS.md. The client currently has two fallback systems for rendering settlements: StreetAlignedPlacement.ts (lot positioning) and StreetNetworkLayout.ts (street rendering), both grid-only. When stored street/lot data exists in the DB, the client already uses it (BabylonGame.ts reads lot positions from DB, and generateStreetAlignedLots accepts existingNetwork). Verify and fix: (1) StreetAlignedPlacement.ts generateStreetAlignedLots(): when existingNetwork is provided (lines 118-166), it converts stored segments to visual streets — verify this works for non-grid waypoints (curved, diagonal, etc.). (2) The fallback path (lines 168+) generates a procedural layout when no DB data exists — keep this as grid fallback but do not override DB data. (3) BabylonGame.ts lot offset computation (lines 4520-4548): verify the waypoint centroid approach works for non-grid layouts where streets aren't axis-aligned. (4) Town square / park placement (lines 5318+): currently uses getCenterBlockBounds() as fallback — verify the DB park lot path works for all patterns. (5) Society preview map (LocationMapPreview): verify it renders stored lot positions correctly regardless of pattern. (6) Typecheck passes. (7) Verify in browser with one settlement of each pattern type.

tasks.title: Ensure SVG settlement preview stays consistent with actual game settlement generation
tasks.description: The SettlementDialog.tsx SVG preview (in client/src/components/dialogs/SettlementDialog.tsx) shows a schematic layout for each Type/Terrain combination. This preview must remain a reliable representation of what the game will actually generate. (1) The SVG preview's pattern selection logic (selectPattern function) must mirror the server-side selectStreetPattern() rules exactly — if the server changes which terrain/type combos produce which patterns, the preview must update to match. Consider extracting shared pattern selection into a shared/ module imported by both. (2) The grid case already matches server constants (SERVER_GRID_SIZE mirrors GRID_SIZE, LOTS_PER_BLOCK=6 mirrors COLS_PER_BLOCK×ROWS_PER_BLOCK). For each non-grid pattern, the SVG must show the same structural layout the server generates: buildings on roads (never offroad), correct block shapes, park placement, and approximate lot count. (3) The lot count displayed (getLotCount()) must match the actual number of lots the server creates for that Type/Terrain. If the server's lot count formula changes, getLotCount must update. (4) For each of the 6 patterns (grid, organic, linear, waterfront, hillside, radial), generate a test settlement in-game and visually compare with the SVG preview — adjust the preview until it is a recognizable miniature of the actual layout. (5) Add a comment block at the top of the SVG preview code referencing the server files it must stay in sync with (street-network-generator.ts, street-generator.ts, geography-generator.ts). (6) Verify in browser.

tasks.title: Update Society preview map to render real geographic coordinates
tasks.description: The Society tab's LocationMapPreview currently uses arbitrary circular layouts. Update it to render actual geographic data. (1) World view: render all countries at their real position coordinates, with territoryPolygon drawn as the country border (replacing the circular border). (2) Country view: render the country's territory polygon as the border, with settlements at their real worldPositionX/worldPositionZ positions inside. (3) Settlement view: unchanged — already uses real lot/street positions. (4) Camera framing at each zoom level computed from actual entity bounds (bounding box of territory polygon, settlement boundary, etc.) instead of hardcoded radii. (5) Inter-settlement roads visible in country view. (6) Transitions between zoom levels animate smoothly. (7) Typecheck passes. (8) Verify in browser.

tasks.title: Update 3D game engine to use stored world coordinates for settlement placement
tasks.description: The 3D game currently computes settlement positions at runtime. Update it to read stored world coordinates so the game world matches the Society map exactly. (1) BabylonGame.ts reads worldPositionX/worldPositionZ from settlement data when placing settlements on the terrain. (2) Settlement radius from the database is used instead of recomputing. (3) Inter-settlement roads are rendered between settlements using the stored road data. (4) Terrain size is set from the world's mapWidth/mapDepth. (5) Player spawns at a position consistent with the world coordinate system. (6) NPC settlement zone centers match the stored world positions. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Show SVG settlement footprints at country zoom level in Society preview
tasks.description: Currently the Society map's country view shows settlements as small dots with labels, then jumps to full 3D detail when clicking into a settlement — the transition is jarring. At the country zoom level, replace the simple dot marker with a miniature SVG settlement layout footprint (reusing the SVG generation logic from SettlementDialog.tsx). This gives users a sense of the settlement's street pattern and density before clicking in. (1) Extract the SVG settlement layout generation logic from SettlementDialog.tsx (the SettlementLayoutPreview component's useMemo block) into a shared utility, e.g. shared/settlement-layout-svg.ts, that returns SVG path/rect data (not React elements) given settlementType, terrain, and lot count. (2) In LocationMapPreview.tsx, at the country zoom level, for each settlement: render the SVG layout as a Babylon.js DynamicTexture applied to a flat plane (CreateGround) positioned at the settlement's world coordinates, scaled to the settlement's radius. The texture is generated by drawing the SVG paths onto a canvas context. (3) The SVG footprint should show streets as lines and buildings as small rectangles, matching the pattern (grid/organic/linear/etc.) and density for that settlement's type and terrain. (4) The SVG footprint planes should be tagged as 'buildings' layer and hidden at world zoom level (too small to see), visible at country zoom level, and replaced by full 3D detail at settlement zoom level. (5) Settlement labels remain above the SVG footprint. (6) Typecheck passes. (7) Verify in browser: at country zoom, each settlement shows a recognizable miniature of its layout; clicking in transitions smoothly to the full 3D detail view.

tasks.title: Add world scale configuration to world creation UI
tasks.description: Allow users to configure geographic scale when creating a world. (1) Add worldScale config option to WorldGenerationConfig: 'compact' | 'standard' | 'expansive' or explicit { settlementRadius, countryRadius, worldRadius } overrides. (2) World creation dialog includes a 'World Scale' option with presets: Compact (settlement spacing 300-500, country radius 600-1000), Standard (spacing 500-1000, country radius 1500-3000), Expansive (spacing 1000-2000, country radius 3000-6000). (3) Custom option allows specifying explicit dimension overrides. (4) Selected scale is stored in generationConfig and used during world generation. (5) Typecheck passes. (6) Verify in browser.
